paper_title: Isotopic Analysis and Firnification Modeling in Ice Cores

methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring the isotopic composition of nitrogen (δ 15 N) in various ice cores using different techniques and instruments to understand past climate conditions.

steps.description: The isotopic composition of nitrogen (δ 15 N) was measured on the NEEM core from 1746.8 to 1811.6 m depth at Laboratoire des Sciences du Climat et de l'Environnement (LSCE), France.
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ 15 N on NEEM core at LSCE

steps.description: A melt-refreeze technique was used to extract the air from the ice.
steps.searchable_summary: apply melt-refreeze technique to extract air from ice

steps.description: The collected air is then measured by dual inlet mass spectrometry (Delta V plus, Thermo Scientific).
steps.searchable_summary: measure air using dual inlet mass spectrometry

steps.description: Data are corrected for mass interferences occurring in the mass spectrometer.
Dry atmospheric air is used as a standard to express the results.
steps.searchable_summary: use dry atmospheric air as standard for results

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the depth differences between temperature increases recorded in the gas and ice phases to infer past surface temperature and accumulation.

steps.description: Assume that δ 15 N peaks and δ 18 Oice peaks are synchronous and relate the maximum firn temperature gradient to the peaks in δ 18 O-ice.
steps.searchable_summary: synchronize δ 15 N and δ 18 Oice peaks to relate firn temperature gradient

steps.description: Calculate depth differences between temperature increases recorded in the gas and ice phases.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate depth differences between gas and ice phase temperature increases

methods.description: This method uses a firnification and heat diffusion model to reconstruct surface temperature scenarios from δ 15 N profiles.

steps.description: Use a semi-empirical firnification model with heat diffusion to calculate initial firn depth, age difference, and temperature gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: apply firnification model to calculate firn depth, age difference, and temperature gradient

steps.description: Calculate δ 15 N as the sum of gravitational and thermal effects.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate δ 15 N from gravitational and thermal effects

methods.method_name: Validation of the Goujon Firnification Model
methods.description: This method validates the Goujon firnification model using present-day characteristics of the firn at NEEM.

steps.description: Use present-day characteristics of the firn at NEEM to validate the Goujon firnification model.
steps.searchable_summary: validate Goujon model with present-day NEEM firn characteristics

steps.description: Calculate gravitational fractionation affecting δ 15 N assuming no thermal effect.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate gravitational fractionation for δ 15 N
